manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: potion, bloke, terrorism, libido, instructor, stairwell, washroom, daybreak, teacher.
Your opponent's team words are: swimsuit, burglary, serenity, allergy, telegram, pimple, geisha, bathrobe.
Distractor words are: snowman, wildlife, drifter, airstrip, cleaver, bikini, drunkard.
The kill words are: albino.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue related to some of our team's words, not using any of the words. Must avoid any kill word (albino) as target not to guess. Also must avoid clue being any of the words (including opponent, distractor, kill). Should produce CLUE and TARGETS list.

We need to decide which target words to relate. Could aim for multiple words like "morning" relates to daybreak, maybe also to teacher? Not sure. "Education" relates to teacher, instructor. "Sex" relates to libido, maybe also bloke? Could be "school" relates to teacher, instructor, maybe daybreak? Not.

We need to be careful not to hint to opponent words like "bathrobe" etc. Also we must avoid kill word "albino" - no issue.

We can give clue "Lesson" - relates to teacher, instructor. Both are in our team. That seems safe. Could also relate to "daybreak" if thinking of "morning lesson". Might be too ambiguous.

Clue "Morning" could relate to daybreak, maybe also to washroom (morning washroom) not strong. Better to stick with two words teacher and instructor.

Clue "Academy" maybe relates to teacher, instructor, maybe also to libido? No. Safer.

Thus CLUE: Academy
TARGETS: teacher, instructor

Check that "academy" not one of the words. Good.

Return formatted answer
